VAL-WATCH-001.evidence: Started mors watch in background, sent message msg_d5e0daa8 and reply msg_187c2b9f. Watch output showed: [📨 New message] with message_id, thread_id, sender, recipient, state; [↩️ Reply] with message_id, thread_id, in_reply_to, sender, recipient, state. All required context fields present.

VAL-WATCH-002.evidence: Sent SIGINT to watch process. Watch printed 'Watch stopped.' and exited cleanly. kill -0 check confirmed process terminated. No orphaned processes found via ps aux grep. Terminal remained usable after exit.

VAL-WATCH-003.evidence: Two pre-existing messages were sent before watch started. After starting watch and waiting 2 seconds, watch output contained only the startup banner 'Watching for new events...' (1 line). No pre-existing message events were emitted. Only post-subscription send/reply events appeared.

VAL-WATCH-004.evidence: Sent message msg_2137ab92, then read it, then acked it during a watch session. Watch output showed exactly 2 events for this message_id: one 📨 New message (create) and one ✅ Acked (state transition). No duplicate emissions for same event transition. Read did not produce a separate watch event (correct per design).

toolsUsed: CLI direct execution (inline - subagent permission error)
